title: Bhatner Fort
tagline: Bhatner Fort — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: The Bhatner Fort was built by son of Rao Bhati, about 419 km northwest of Jaipur along the old Delhi–Multan road and 230 km north-east of Bikaner.
overview.description: The Bhatner Fort was built by son of Rao Bhati, about 419 km northwest of Jaipur along the old Delhi–Multan road and 230 km north-east of Bikaner. Another name of Hanumangarh was Bhatner, which means "fortress of the Bhati". Believed to be 1700 years old, it is considered to be one of the oldest forts of India.

topPlaces.name: Hanumangarh
topPlaces.description: Hanumangarh is a city and municipal council in the Indian state of Rajasthan, situated on the banks of the river Ghaggar, located about 400 km from Delhi. It is the administrative headquarter of Hanumangarh district.

items.note: Hanumangarh is a city and municipal council in the Indian state of Rajasthan, situated on the banks of the river Ghaggar, located about 400 km from Delhi. It is the administrative headquarter of Hanumangarh district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bhatner Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bhatner Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bhatner Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bikaner Airport (Nal) (201 km) and the nearest railway station is Bikaner Junction (201 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bhatner Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bhatner Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bhatner Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Bhatner Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
